rag_rules: AGREE diagnostics pointing to unresolved identifiers or invalid helpers should be fixed by tracing and correcting the root reference or removing the invalid clause.
When AGREE reports type mismatches, align helper declarations with the actual expression types (bool, int, real).
Avoid generating clauses that compare Boolean, enumeration, or opaque data types using numeric operations; use only valid Boolean expressions.
generic_rules: Validator diagnostics should be resolved by identifying and correcting root causes without introducing new architecture elements.
AGREE contracts must use only visible identifiers and helpers declared in the same annex scope.
Ensure that property associations and references in AGREE contracts match the AADL model's configured dependencies.
Component type contracts should not reference implementation-specific ports or subcomponents unless properly represented.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts are enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Assumptions and guarantees referring to component ports must be placed in valid component type scopes.
Implementation-level bindings should be placed in component implementation scopes after relevant subcomponents and connections.
Generated AGREE code must not introduce package-qualified identifiers or property references not present in the analyzed model.
anti_patterns: Do not add new architecture elements to resolve AGREE diagnostics; instead, correct or remove invalid clauses.
Do not attempt to convert Boolean or opaque data types into numeric comparisons unless the AADL model supports it.
Do not reference identifiers or properties that are not visible in the current AADL scope or configured dependencies.
irrelevant_topics: AADL Inspector diagnostics about property associations not pointing to anything
Component type annexes vs implementation annexes (not relevant to repair pattern)
